tags.name: Oval top with Rounded Shoulders and Complex Checks
Square Top with Double Rounded Shoulders

stones.title: Children of Thomas and Elinor Davies
stones.notes: In memory of the children of Thomas and Elinor Davies. Catharine [name partly worn] died April 27, 1852, aged 1 year, 2 months and 5 weeks. Mary died January 26, 1860, aged 1 month. Richard died November 26, 1863, aged 6 months and 2 weeks.
stones.trans:
ER COF AM
Plant Thomas ac Elinor Davies.
Catharine [?] a fu farw Ebrill 27, 1852,
yn 1 flwydd, 2 fis, a 5 wythnos oed.
Mary a fu farw Ion. 26, 1860. Yn 1
mis oed.
Richard a fu farw Tach. 26, 1863.
Yn 6 mis, 2 wythnos oed.

stones.title: William Roberts
stones.trans:
IN
MEMORY
OF
WILLIAM ROBERTS,
SON OF
JOHN & ELENOR ROBERTS,
WHO DEPARTED THIS LIFE
JULY 8th 1865,
AGED 27 YEARS & 8 MONTHS.

stones.title: William Lloyd
stones.notes:
Englyn

In a snug cottage he was born,
In California's land;
In Havre de Grace he died,
Soon after reaching shore;
And here he lies at rest,
Quiet in the grave,
Until the morning he is raised
To live in the land of peace.
stones.trans:
[DRAFT] ER COF
Am
William mab Robert ac Eleanor Lloyd,
yr hwn a fu farw Gorphenaf 17 A.D. 1859,
yn 1 flwydd, 9 mis a 14 diwrnod oed.

Mewn bwthyn clyd fe'i ganwyd,
Yn Nghalifornia dir;
Yn Havre de Grace bu farw,
Yn ebrwydd 'rol cyrhaedd tir;
Ac yma mae yn gorphwys,
Yn dawel yn y bedd,
Hyd foreu fe'i hadferir
I fyw i wlad yr hedd.

stones.title: Owen K. Thomas
stones.notes:
Englyn

My beloved spouse sleeps here;
I shall seek him no more,
Alas, he will not return,
His strength spent in a bed of earth.
stones.trans:
ER COF AM
OWEN K. THOMAS.
Gynt o Bethesda, Arfon.
Bu farw Medi 8, 1883.
Oed 56 mlwydd.

Fy mhriod hynod sy'n huno, yma
Wnir mwyach ei geisio;
Ataf ni ddychwel eto,
Gwael ei rym mewn gwely o ro.

ER COF AM
CATHRINE THOMAS
Ganwyd Awyst 1826
Bu farw Chwefrol 1918

stones.title: Ellen Evans
stones.trans:
IN
MEMORY
OF
ELLEN WIFE OF ROBERT E. EVANS
WHO DEPARTED THIS LIFE
AUGt 7, A.D. 1861.
AGED 30 YEARS.

stones.title: Susie Griffiths
stones.trans:
IN MEMORY OF
SUSIE
WIFE OF
Griffith Griffiths.
Born Feb 6, 1865.
Died Oct 10, 1889.
Gone but not forgotten.

stones.title: John Evans
stones.notes: In memory of John, son of Evan and Cathrine Evans, born November 23, 1859, died June 30, 1865.
stones.trans:
ER COF
AM JOHN,
mab Evan a Cathrine Evans,
ganwyd Tach. 23, 1859,
bu farw Meh. 30, 1865.

stones.title: Jane Thomas
stones.notes:
In memory of Jane, wife of Thomas J. Thomas, died November 24, 1880, aged 32 years.

"He weakened my strength in the way; he shortened my days." (Psalm 102:23)
stones.trans:
ER COF AM
JANE
PRIOD THOMAS J. THOMAS
BU FARW
TACHWEDD 24, 1880.
OED 32 MLWYDD.

Gostyngodd efe fy nerth ar y ffordd;
byrhaodd fy nyddiau.

stones.title: Ann Jane Williams
stones.trans:
IN
MEMORY
OF
ANN JANE,
Daughter of
Griffith & Jane Williams,
Who was born May 12th 1854,
died Sept 25th 1859.

stones.title: Crafts Family
stones.notes: Pedestal monument inscribed on multiple faces; CRAFTS carved on the base course on at least two sides.
stones.trans:
MOSES CRAFTS
1792  1873

SARAH CRAFTS
His Wife
1796  1889

[second face:]
ABBY,
Wife Of
ALDEN B. CRAFTS
1817  1873

[base:] CRAFTS

stones.title: Carter Family
stones.notes: Stacked pedestal monument within a slate-kerbed family plot.
stones.trans:
CLARK CARTER,
1804 - 1874.
HANNAH BODWELL,
HIS WIFE,
1801 - 1889.

[base:] CARTER

stones.title: Juliana Carlson
stones.trans:
JULIANA CARLSON
Born March 15, 1851
Died Sept. 25, 1890

stones.title: Unknown
stones.notes: Numbered plot marker — no name. Likely a cemetery lot number; Monson's slate industry supplied these small numbered posts.

stones.title: Christensen Family
stones.notes: The epitaph quotes the hymn "Sometime We'll Understand" (Maxwell Cornelius, 1891) — its popularity dates the carving no earlier than the 1890s.
stones.trans:
ANDERS
CHRISTENSON
Born 1850
Died 1905

ADDIE L. his wife
Born 1863
Died 1895

Sometime
we will understand

[base:] CHRISTENSON

stones.title: Smith Family
stones.notes: Inscription cut within a recessed, honed panel — a distinctly Monson treatment.
stones.trans:
BION B. SMITH
1859  -  1946
LORA L. HIS WIFE
1869  -  1936
NORA N. BUTTERFIELD
1869  -  1942

stones.title: Stevens
stones.notes: Modern pictorial slate: sandblasted treeline, cabin, and canoeist, with a full-moon piercing cut through the stone — a contemporary continuation of the Monson slate tradition (a small plaque reads Bob Stevens).
stones.trans:
STEVENS

Here on these beloved hills
that he loved so much
his life has come full circle
His roots run deep on this spot
In Monson, Maine

Husband, Father, Grandfather
and Best Friend

stones.title: Carlson Family
stones.notes: Lower lines partly hidden behind memorial flowers in the photograph — the bracketed passages need verification on a return visit.
stones.trans:
[cross]
CARLSON

OSCAR HELMER
JUNE 11, 1881 - FEB. 27, 1948
LOVISA JULI[A]NA
HIS [WIFE]
FEB. 2, 1875 - [...] 1962

stones.title: Elwin Family
stones.notes: "PC" incised at the lower right is likely a maker's mark — worth tracking across other Monson stones for carver attribution. Note Daniel, an infant (1950–1950).
stones.trans:
HAROLD ELWIN
1923  -  2001
WIFE
ALICE ALTHEA HAGGSTROM
1927  -  2011
SON
DANIEL ELWIN
1950  -  1950

PC

stones.title: French
stones.notes: Name-only family stone with an engraved loon and overhanging branch — modern Monson work; compare the Stevens stone's pictorial style.

stones.title: Sally Hayden
stones.notes: MIxed mixed folk-art iconography with heart over urn

stones.title: Martha Green
stones.notes: Slate source was Pin Hill Quarry, Carver was the Park Family from Groton, MA
